title: Began My Descent Into Code
description: Started with Visual Basic, C#, and C/C++. Learned how memory management works, mostly by accidentally leaking it, and figured out how to make software yell directly at hardware.

title: Playing with Fire (and Motherboards)
description: Developed a knack for chip-level laptop repairs. Spent my time reballing BGAs, sniffing out short circuits on power rails, and breathing in toxic soldering fumes instead of replacing boards like a normal person.

title: Discovered the World Wide Web
description: Accidentally wandered into HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Built my first personal projects and realized that networking fundamentals are just computers shouting at each other across a room.

title: Breaking Windows Legally
description: Deep dived into Windows internals and reverse engineering. Began writing kernel-mode drivers, giving my computer a brand new, highly efficient way to instantly trigger a Blue Screen of Death.

title: Infiltrated Acom IT
description: Convinced Acom Information Technology to hire me as a Full-Stack Developer & SysAdmin. Took over their web apps, server stacks, and network infrastructure before they could change their minds.

title: The SysAdmin / Dev Survival Era
description: Spent 3 long years at Acom IT managing servers, treating network infrastructure like a fragile house of cards, and keeping web platforms alive. Gained massive real-world experience because everything was constantly trying to break.

title: The Escape to Cyber Yakku
description: Left Acom IT to take a senior role at Cyber Yakku PVT LTD. Immediately began complicating my life further by diving deeper into HWID authentication systems, kernel exploitation, and mobile apps.

title: Senior Overthinker & Project Manager @ Cyber Yakku
description: Currently leading teams and driving software strategy. This means I'm responsible for mobile architecture, systems engineering, and managing project timelines that clients desperately try to shorten. I mostly manage cross-functional chaos.

title: Fluttering Around Mobile Dev
description: Started learning Flutter for iOS and Android. Combined it with Firebase backends and real-time geolocation tracking so I can pinpoint exactly where my code is currently crashing a user's phone.

title: Dansal App — 800K+ Unsuspecting Users
description: Launched the Dansal App for Vesak festival tracking. Shockingly reached 800,000+ downloads and was featured on national radio (Sha FM), confirming that Sri Lanka has a massive appetite for free food and my apps.

title: ExcellentComputers.lk — Active E-Commerce
description: Built and deployed a full production e-commerce platform for a computer hardware retailer. It is live, it accepts real currency, and it successfully trades computer parts for money without breaking.

title: The Alpha Platform — Officially Haunted
description: Launched a full SaaS platform at thealpha.vip. Implemented cryptographic license protection and hardware-ID locking to keep out freeloading users who try to outsmart my licensing algorithms.

title: Exposing My Code via Open Source
description: Bravely pushed two tools to GitHub: Weedr (a secure qBittorrent wrapper) and APK-Wooper (a self-hosted workspace to rip apart Android files). Feel free to judge the source code.

title: Paranoia, Hypervisors, & AI Research
description: Moving further into the dark arts of hypervisor research, anti-detection engines, and custom driver communication. Also sprinkling in some AI automation to see if a machine can write bugs faster than I can.
